query: Is Canberra the capital of Australia
answer: Canberra is the capital of Australia. It was established in 1908 as the nation's administrative center. It is located in the Australian Capital Territory.

results.title: Canberra, Australia | Geography and Cartography | Research Starters | EBSCO Research
results.snippet: Canberra, the capital of Australia and the Australian Capital Territory (ACT), is a uniquely planned city established to serve as the nation's administrative center. Developed after the ACT's formation in 1908, Canberra spans approximately 805.6 square kilometers (311 square miles) and is located between Sydney and Melbourne, surrounded by natural landscapes, including bushlands and the Brindabella mountain range. Canberra is organized into seven districts, each with its own town center, and includes notable landmarks within the Parliamentary Triangle, such as Parliament House and Lake Burley Griffin. Canberra is the capital of Australia and the Australian Capital Territory (ACT). Near City and the Parliamentary Triangle is Lake Burley Griffin, an artificial lake created from the Molonglo River, which bisected Canberra before it was dammed off. In 1908, 2,358 square kilometers (910 square miles) of grazing land in the Yass-Canberra region of New South Wales was designated Federal Capital Territory.

results.snippet: # What is the capital of Australia? **The capital of Australia is Canberra**. Even though the grand opening ceremony was to be held in Melbourne, **no city had yet been designated as the capital of Australia**. Faced with widespread chaos, a radical decision was made to cut to the chase: they **would build a new city from the ground up!** Australia’s first capital city built from the ground up. ## Canberra, Australia’s small capital city. But entertaining highlights were not limited to those years in Australia’s new capital city. When spring returns, **over 500,000 visitors flock straight to Australia’s capital city to admire the sea of flowers surrounding Lake Burley Griffin**. And this is the brief history of how Canberra became Australia’s capital city. results.snippet: # Where is Canberra. # Where is Canberra. Canberra is Australia’s capital city. You’ll find us in south east Australia, in the Australian Capital Territory (ACT). Australia’s two most populated cities are nearby – Sydney to the north and Melbourne to the south. In 1901, this region was chosen as the setting for Australia’s new capital city. Australia was – and is – a young nation, and wanted a new, modern city as its capital. Canberra and it’s surrounding regions combined make a population of nearer to 950,000. As connected with the world as we are detached from the bustle and rushing you’ll find in Australia’s bigger cities. But over time, and as Canberra has emerged, the work here has changed. ## Life in Canberra. No matter where you live in Canberra, it’s easy to find your way to nature. You can also fly in from Australia’s other capital cities and regional centres or book a seat on a coach or rail service from Sydney or Melbourne.
